ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Son Goku' and official character tag is 'Son Goku: GT' per DBS card database. The depicted child in orange gi with dark spiky hair gathering ki energy matches Goku's canonical child-form design. Note: the official tag is 'Son Goku: GT' (Dragon Ball GT era child Goku, not classic Dragon Ball child Goku) — important distinction for the character layer.

trivia_bullets.text: Pint-sized Warrior Son Goku (BT3-006) is a red Common from Cross Worlds (DBS-B03), released March 9, 2018 — the third main DBS Card Game booster set. BT3 introduced black as a new color and focused on diverse deck construction as its primary design theme [Source: dbs-cardgame.com/europe-en/strategy/cross-worlds/designers-note.php].

BT3-006's skill: '[Auto] When you play this card, look at up to 7 cards from the top of your deck. Choose up to 1 red Son Goku: GT among them and add it to your hand. Then shuffle your deck.' The card's official character tag is 'Son Goku: GT' — placing this child-form Goku within the Dragon Ball GT continuity, where Goku is restored to child form by the Black Star Dragon Balls. The card functions as a deep-dig tutor for GT Goku variants [Source: WebSearch snippet from coretcg.com BT3-006 listing; alternateworldscomics.com BT3-006 listing].

In Dragon Ball GT (1996-1997 anime), Goku is accidentally wished back to child form by the Black Star Dragon Balls, retaining all his adult combat knowledge and power in a child's body — a reversal of the classic 'small but mighty' premise that launched the original Dragon Ball. The 'Pint-sized Warrior' title references this directly [Source: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball GT; CBR: Dragon Ball GT overview].
